mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate, which mechanically compresses the present value of future equity cash flows and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers institutional risk-off rebalancing, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional predictive rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding consumer credit availability measured by RSAFS, when combined with stable industrial production from INDPRO, indicates healthy demand without supply bottlenecks, which supports corporate margin expansion and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any rolling 12-month period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, rising industrial production from INDPRO demonstrates resilient manufacturing capacity that sustains corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative in 60% of instances where INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, or the forward return distribution shows no significant skew toward positive outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent headline inflation exceeding a 3.5% year-over-year threshold forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy stances, which elevates equity risk premiums and depresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold, or if the forward return median does not differ significantly from the unconditional median (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls from PAYEMS and rising housing starts from HOUST reflects synchronized labor and housing market strength, which amplifies consumer spending momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a five-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a negative or zero information coefficient over any 18-month rolling window, or forward returns fail to exceed the baseline median by at least 0.5% in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in consumer credit balances from RSAFS reflects expanding household purchasing power, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The forward return distribution for months with rising RSAFS does not exhibit a statistically significant positive mean compared to months with falling RSAFS (t-test p > 0.05), or the predictive rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime, a persistently elevated unemployment rate combined with flat real GDP growth from GDPC1 indicates structural labor slack and weak aggregate demand, which suppresses equity earnings revisions and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE remains above 4.0% and GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or if the cross-sectional predictive power vanishes over a 2-year rolling window.
